issue_title: SAML Encryption Key Export Downloads Signing Key
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

When exporting a newly generated SAML encryption key, the /certificates/saml.signing/download api is called instead of /certificates/saml.encryption/download

### Version

21.0.1

### Expected behavior

The encryption certificate should be downloaded


### Actual behavior

the signing certificate is downloaded


### How to Reproduce?

create SAML client
generate encryption and signing keys
exporting either one always downloads the signing-key

### Anything else?

as a workaround the developer tools in chrome can be used to resend the POST Request to the correct endpoint.
commit_message: Call correct SAML key download API (#23375)

issue_title: CLI args not recognized when running Quarkus dev mode
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

Running:

```
mvn -f quarkus/server/pom.xml compile quarkus:dev -Dquarkus.args='start-dev --features=preview'
```

Does not enable preview features.

The reason is that the build steps to configure the `Profile` are executed before calling `KeycloakMain`. See https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/quarkus/runtime/src/main/java/org/keycloak/quarkus/runtime/KeycloakMain.java.

It was initially reported by @jonkoops and I'm not sure what is the change that introduced this issue. Perhaps related to the last Quarkus upgrade or by #19251.

### Version

22.0.3

### Expected behavior

Quarkus dev is working using CLI options.

### Actual behavior

Quarkus dev is not working using CLI options.

### How to Reproduce?

```
mvn -f quarkus/server/pom.xml compile quarkus:dev -Dquarkus.args='start-dev --features=preview'
```

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
CLI args not recognized when running Quarkus dev mode (#23254)

Closes #23253

issue_title: Race condition inside Keycloak build chain execution
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

As the build items might be executed in parallel and the order doesn't have to be ensured, a race condition can happen inside Keycloak build chain execution. 

I've spotted the issue on Win machine during the execution of `quarkus/deployment` tests.

The Crypto provided must be initialized/set before execution of the initialization of the Keycloask Session factory and present factories. Otherwise, the startup crashes.


### Version

999

### Expected behavior

No startup crash

### Actual behavior

Unable to start the server

### How to Reproduce?

Add Thread sleep to the KeycloakProcessor#setCryptoProvider, and the build fails during execution of the KeycloakProcessor#configureKeycloakSessionFactory.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Race condition inside Keycloak build chain execution (#23260)

Fixes #23258

issue_title: admin-fine-grained-authz + client authorization settings requires more realm-management roles
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

When using the preview feature admin-fine-grained-authz to allow certain users to manage their clients, some clients are using the Authorization capabilities. In Keycloak <=v19 using the old Admin UI Console, the only realm-management client roles that were needed to these users were: `manage-authorization`, `query-clients`, `query-users`, `query-groups`. In the new Admin UI Console, the role `view-clients` is required to do anything in the Authorization settings for the client. 

This is a problem as `view-clients` role allows users to view other clients and the client secrets of all applications.

### Version

v22.0.1

### Expected behavior

When using admin-fine-grained-authz settings users with manage permissions to a client should be able to configure and create authorization policies for their client without requiring access to ALL clients in the realm. 

### Actual behavior

To manage/view Authorization settings for a client, the user must have `view-clients` realm management client role.

### How to Reproduce?

1)  Follow instructions at [Fine Grain Admin Permissions](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/#_fine_grain_permissions) to configure a user to manage one specific client.
2) Add realm-management client role of `manage-authorization` to the user as well.
3) Turn on Authorization for the client.
4) Login as user to the security-admin-console for the realm.
5) View the configured managed client and select the Authorization tab.
6) Select any sub-tab of Authorization - Settings, Resources, Scopes, Policies, Permissions, Evaluate, Export
You'll be met with an error message: `Forbidden, permission needed: view-clients`

### Anything else?

![keycloak-authz-resources-tab](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/72021941/023673e3-9fd1-4c61-bc9b-20c68a295cbb)
commit_message:
Allow full use of Authorization tab for anyone with manage-authorization (#23983)

* Allow full use of Authorization tab for anyone with manage-authorization
access.

* Allow export with manage-authorization role.

* manage-authorization makes more sense for the subtabs

issue_title: NPE in AuthenticationManager backchannelLogout
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

In a customer project we observed UncaughtServerErrors which where caused by NPEs being thrown in [AuthenticationManager#backchannelLogout](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/managers/AuthenticationManager.java#L291). This is caused by accessing the user variable obtained from `userSession.getUser()` without a proper null check.

### Version

22.0.1

### Expected behavior

Should logout `null` for the username instead of throwing a NPE.

### Actual behavior

BackchannelLogout sometimes yields an UncaughtServerError based on an NPE in AuthenticationManager#backchannelLogout.

### How to Reproduce?

Hard to reproduce, as we need to create the sitation where the userSession is still present but the user object is gone.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Prevent NPE in AuthenticationManager.backchannelLogout (#23306)

Previously, if the user was already removed from the userSession
and the log level was set to DEBUG, then an NPE was triggered by
the debug log statement during backchannelLogout.

Fixes #23306

issue_title: PolicyEnforcer should set WWW-Authenticate header before calling sendError() method
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

authorization-services

### Describe the bug

Actually the PolicyEnforcer set the www-authenticate/location header after calling the sendError() method:

```java
...
if (ticket != null) {
   response.sendError(401);
   response.setHeader("WWW-Authenticate", new StringBuilder("UMA realm=\"").append(authzClient.getConfiguration().getRealm()).append("\"").append(",as_uri=\"")
                        .append(authzClient.getServerConfiguration().getIssuer()).append("\"").append(",ticket=\"").append(ticket).append("\"").toString());
}
...
```
By doing this way, the header is not added to the response because it has been marked as `commited` by the sendError() method. 

### Version

latest

### Expected behavior

WWW-Authenticate/Location Headers set by the PolicyEnforcer should be returned in the HTTP response to the client.

### Actual behavior

WWW-Authenticate/Location Headers set by the PolicyEnforcer are not returned in the HTTP response to the client because the response has already been committed.

### How to Reproduce?

- Call a Spring endpoint protected by the PolicyEnforcer (see https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak-quickstarts/blob/latest/spring/rest-authz-resource-server).
- Set logger to debug level
- Call the endpoint with a token requiring an UMA permission ticket, the policy enforcer should log `Sending challenge` and the client will not get a 401 response with the WWW-Authenticate header containing the UMA ticket data.


### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Set headers before calling sendError() method

Closes #23325

2. Try to create user as an admin

issue_title: CVE-2023-42503 - Improper Input Validation vulnerability in org.apache.commons:commons-compress
issue_body:
# Overview

[org.apache.commons:commons-compress](https://github.com/apache/commons-compress) is an API for working with compression and archive formats.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Input Validation when parsing TAR files. An attacker can manipulate file modification times headers, leading to a denial of service issue via CPU consumption.

**Note:** This is only exploitable if applications are using the `CompressorStreamFactory` class (with auto-detection of file types), `TarArchiveInputStream`, and `TarFile` classes to parse TAR files.

# Remediation

Upgrade `org.apache.commons:commons-compress` to version 1.24.0 or higher.

# References

- [Apache Advisory](https://lists.apache.org/thread/5xwcyr600mn074vgxq92tjssrchmc93c)
- [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/apache/commons-compress/commit/43354810a9aa4305bf82318f1884e83a078a621a)

# Additional comments

- After analyzing the issue, it appears that the transitive dependency comes from the following dependency tree::

```
+- io.fabric8:kubernetes-junit-jupiter:jar:6.7.2:test
[INFO] |  +- io.fabric8:kubernetes-client:jar:6.7.2:compile
[INFO] |  |  +- io.fabric8:kubernetes-httpclient-okhttp:jar:6.7.2:runtime
[INFO] |  |  |  +- com.squareup.okhttp3:okhttp:jar:3.14.9:runtime
[INFO] |  |  |  |  \- com.squareup.okio:okio:jar:1.17.2:runtime
[INFO] |  |  |  \- com.squareup.okhttp3:logging-interceptor:jar:3.14.9:runtime
[INFO] |  |  \- io.fabric8:zjsonpatch:jar:0.3.0:compile
[INFO] |  \- org.apache.commons:commons-compress:jar:1.23.0:compile
```

As we discussed earlier, `kubernetes-junit-jupiter` is only for testing purposes. However, the package is included in the current image of the Operator. For more details, refer to the Trivy vulnerability report below:

```
❯ trivy image -f json --timeout 25m --quiet --ignore-unfixed quay.io/keycloak/keycloak-operator:22.0.3 | jq '.Results[].Vulnerabilities[]? | "\(.VulnerabilityID) - \(.PkgName) - \(.PkgPath) - \(.Title)"'
"CVE-2023-42503 - org.apache.commons:commons-compress - opt/keycloak/lib/main/org.apache.commons.commons-compress-1.23.0.jar - Improper Input Validation, Uncontrolled Resource Consumption vulnerabi ..."
```

issue_title: The "invalid_grant" error occurs again when loading the offline client session with an early creation time.
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

infinispan

### Describe the bug

#### Background
The origin issue was https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/9959 and wax fixed by https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/17490.

Regarding to the loading client session, I also proposed a fix https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/8671 long time ago.

From that time I made those customization in our production, and everything works well.

Recently, We update our Keycloak version to `22.0.3` and replace the customization with that fix.

And the `invaid_grant` error occurs again.

I compared the code between my customization  and  keycloak implementation, and find that,
there is `checkExpiration` added in the `importClientSession` method

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/model/infinispan/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/sessions/infinispan/InfinispanUserSessionProvider.java#L1072-L1080

#### Issue
When refreshing token, there was error `invalid_grant` and error description is `Session doesn't have required client`
The offline client session fails to load properly, while the offline user session loads successfully.

### Version

22.0.3

### Expected behavior

Offline client session loads successfully.



### Actual behavior

When refreshing token, there was error `invalid_grant` and error description is `Session doesn't have required client`

### How to Reproduce?

The steps are same as the https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/9959

And, the `timestamp` for the offline client session in the database should be a time **before the offline session idle time** or offline max session time (if set)

### Anything else?

I have identified the problem and will submit a PR later.
commit_message:
Fix unexpected expiration when import offline client session

Closes #23397

issue_title: After the recent switch to resteasy-reactive we are unable to use resteasy-classic or jersey jax-rs clients.
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

dependencies

### Describe the bug

We have a number of custom SPI's which are using jax-rs client and more specifically currently Resteasy "classic". 
After the switch to resteasy-reactive we are unable to start Quarkus build due to various initialization issues and collisions.
* We tried to find and use resteasy-reactive but looks like the only one provided is in Quarkus which is and extensions and currently not enabled in Keycloak.
* We tried to switch the Jersey, but we still failed to build Quarkus image because of issues on the classloader. Looks like Jersey is not implementing some things but also we are getting same ServiceLoader issues ` Caused by: io.quarkus.builder.BuildException: Build failure: Build failed due to errors
     [exec]     [error]: Build step io.quarkus.resteasy.reactive.server.deployment.ResteasyReactiveProcessor#setupEndpoints threw an exception: java.util.ServiceConfigurationError: jakarta.ws.rs.ext.RuntimeDelegate: org.glassfish.jersey.internal.RuntimeDelegateImpl not a subtype

The options we have left is switch find completely standalone client or maybe keycloak can enable the resteasy-reactive extension.

23.0.0

issue_title: Docs: authorization_services/topics/service-authorization-obtaining-permission.adoc
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

authorization-services

### Describe the bug



File: authorization_services/topics/service-authorization-obtaining-permission.adoc


In the "Obtaining permissions" chapter, there is an optional field name "claim_token", according to the document it is "A string representing additional claims that should be considered by the server when evaluating permissions for the resource(s) and scope(s) being requested"

So basically, by using a regex policy, I would have expected to been able to check the string provided in the claim_token matche with the regex. But unfortunately, it seems that regex policy only look at user attribute.

This stackoverflow post is related to the problem we're facing:
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/75855902/pushing-claims-to-regex-based-policy-in-keycloak



### Version

22.0.3

### Expected behavior

<!-- describe what you want to see in the docs -->
How to use a "claim_token" in a regex policy ?

### Actual behavior

<!-- describe what is currently wrong or missing in the docs -->
regex policy is only using user attribute

### How to Reproduce?

<!-- list steps in the application that show behavior that should be documented -->
- create a regex policy with he following params:
  name: destination
  target claim: destination
  regex pattern: ^desk.+$

- Send to the request to KC, with the following params:
  "claim_token_format": "urn:ietf:params:oauth:token-type:jwt" 
  "claim_token": '{"destination":["deskA"]}' // encoded in base64


permission using the "destination" policy is always denied (unless we add a "destination" attribute to the user). In any case, the value from "claim_token" is not used to evaluate the regex

### Anything else?

- Is there a setting to do in KC to use the claim_token value in regexp policy ?

- Do we have to create a custom "regex policy" looking at the `Attributes attributes = evaluation.getContext().getAttributes();` (claim_token value) ?

- any other suggestion ?

issue_title: User cannot be created via Admin UI if custom user attribute is required
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

When having the user-profile feature enabled and having a custom user attribute marked as required, admin cannot create user via the Admin UI because the attribute is not displayed in the user creation screen, therefore cannot be provided. The field is only shown when editing user and can be set there (have to make the attribute optional)...



### Version

nightly

### Expected behavior

Admin is able to create user via Admin UI when having custom user attributes marked as required.

### Actual behavior

Admin is not able to create user via Admin UI when having custom user attributes marked as required.

### How to Reproduce?

- Enable the user-profile and add new custom user attribute (for e.g locale) and mark it as required
- Try to create user as an admin

### Anything else?

For implementing this a new endpoint for accessing the user profile metadata would become necessary, as the user details API can not be used. It would make sense to afterwards also use that API on the user details screen and decommission the old way.

On the create user screen no inputs for user profile attributes are rendered - if one is required the user can not be created of course. The issue seems to be that for the UserForm to render the UserProfileFields also a user has to be present at the moment:
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/d8ff73e34f4cbaae18b9926a42d22a0aeda74854/js/apps/admin-ui/src/user/UserForm.tsx#L226-L228
The user is undefined in case of create user.

imho this is a general flaw of the user screens gathering the user profile metadata through the user details api. Instead maybe a dedicated endpoint in the ui-ext extension should be added, allowing for users with view-users or manage-users permissions to gather the user profile metadata for admin ui (create and edit user - no user entity necessary). When this would be refactored, it could also be addressed, that there is a [discrepancy between what the frontend thinks it gets and what the backend actually returns](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/23443) for user profile metadata, resulting in user profile attribute validators to not work in the admin ui.

Afterwards the additional parameter and response data can be removed from the users api ...

issue_title: NullPointerException in SAML IdP Logout request with SessionIndex and without NameID
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

identity-brokering

### Describe the bug

Based on Section 3.7.1 of [Assertions and Protocols for the OASIS
Security Assertion Markup Language
(SAML) V2.0 – Errata Composite](https://www.oasis-open.org/committees/download.php/35711/sstc-saml-core-errata-2.0-wd-06-diff.pdf), BaseID or NameID or EncryptedID must be present in the LogoutRequest. SessionIndex is optional.
Keycloak now assumes that NameID always exists.

This throw a NullPointerException for SAML IdP Logout request with SessionIndex and without NameID (BaseID  or EncryptedID) exists. Keycloak must check that at least one of BaseID, NameID and EncryptedID exists and after that if SessionIndex exists, logout request will be handled based on SessionIndex.
As mentioned in the document: 

> If no <SessionIndex> elements are supplied, then all sessions associated with the principal MUST be
invalidated

### Version

22.0.3

### Expected behavior

SAML IdP Logout request with SessionIndex and without NameID (BaseID  or EncryptedID) will succeed to logout User.

### Actual behavior

NullPointerException

### How to Reproduce?

Send Logout Request from a SAML IdP that support it and send SessionIndex together with BaseID  or EncryptedID (fe https://idp.admin.grnet.gr/idp/shibboleth)

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
successful SAML IdP Logout Request with BaseID or EncryptedID and SessionIndex

Closes #23528

Signed-off-by: cgeorgilakis-grnet <cgeorgilakis@admin.grnet.gr>

issue_title: Can't start Keycloak 22.0.3 on Windows
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

I can't start Keycloak 22.0.3 on Windows 10. 
I also checked the latest nightly release, and the same problem happens.

```
Unrecognized option: -
Error: Could not create the Java Virtual Machine.
Error: A fatal exception has occurred. Program will exit.
```

I copied the `kc.bat` file from version 21.0.1, and it works successfully. 
It seems that something changed, and it broke the start process.

### Version

22.0.3

### Expected behavior

Successful start of Keycloak 22.0.3 via `kc.bat`.

### Actual behavior

Error during start of Keycloak 22.0.3 via `kc.bat`.

### How to Reproduce?

`bin\kc.bat start`

### Anything else?

I checked the latest changes to kc.bat. There I found a commit: [ "Removed lots of unnecessary quotes.."](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/1c9992fdaeff9566fd8f734ec5fdbf82c7fb5720). Maybe not all of them are unnecessary. Because Windows commands fail when you call directory including spaces. We need quotes for these cases.
I am not sure whether this is the actual reason, but it may be one of the possible causes.

issue_title: Default-Value in UI for krbPrincipalAttribute is error prone
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

Keycloak Admin-UI has a default-Value for krbPrincipalAttribute:
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/57e51e9dd425d5a9da0064d6380b31ac497f9537/js/apps/admin-ui/src/user-federation/ldap/LdapSettingsKerberosIntegration.tsx#L209

In case your LDAP does not have an Attribute containing the Kerberos Principal-Name this field has to be empty to use the alternative way to lookup the ldap entry (just with the username without domain).

But in this case if you edit the storage provider in the UI,  the UI will autmatically fill in "userPrincipalName", despite the field should be empty. So this does not reflect the actual setting.
Even worse if you edit another attribute in the UI and save without removing it again. Then the user is altering the krbPrincipalAttribute (setting userPrincipalName) without even noticing the change.

Therefore I suggest to remove the default in this line.


### Version

22.0.3

### Expected behavior

Ldap UI reflects the actual setting as configured and no attribute will be altered without noticing.

### Actual behavior

UI does not show the actual configuration (in case of empty...) and overwrites the empty value with a (most likely wrong) default in case of saving.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Add an LDAP  User Federation with Kerberos integration.
2. Clear the field of krbPrincipalAttribute.
3. Save
4. Close the Input mask (e.g. Click in the lefthand menu to User Federation
5. Monitor network traffic in browser
6. Go back to the previously added User Federation
7. now you can see "userPrincipalName" (but in reality the value is not set - seen for example in HTTP response)
8. save
9. Close the input mask
10. Go back to the previously added User Federation again
11. now you can see "userPrincipalName" (and this time it is also set to "userPrincipalName" in the HTTP response)

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Join group screen does not respect pagination settings without filters
issue_body: ### Before reporting an issue - [X] I have searched existing issues - [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly) ### Area admin/ui ### Describe the bug We've noticed this happening since our upgrade to Keycloak 22(.0.3), but it could be older. When clicking on "Join Groups" in the Admin UI, either within the user creation page or on the Groups tab of a user's page, an incorrect number of items is returned. Location of the two Join Groups buttons we're talking about. ### Version 22.0.3, but tested on last night's nightly ### Expected behavior When clicking Join groups, we see the first 10 groups Expected list of groups ### Actual behavior When clicking Join groups, we see the first 3 groups Actual list of groups shown, even though a 10-long page was requested ### How to Reproduce? 1. Spin-up an instance of Keycloak nightly 2. Create a new realm 3. Create the following group structure: - A - 'Child of A-`x`' for x=1 through 5 (5 subgroups in total) - B - 'Child of B-`x`' for x=1 through 5 (5 subgroups in total) - C ... E with 5 child subgroups each - F ... I with no subgroups 4. Create a user 5. Click Join groups on either the user creation page or the user's page - Groups tab. ### Anything else? This is loosely related to https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/23582. I believe the reason why only three groups show instead of the existing 5 is that the request to `/groups` takes subgroups into account even though they're not shown in the UI. This is why to us, this is its own issue separate from the first one.

issue_title: Join group screen does not respect or allow pagination with a filter
issue_body: ### Before reporting an issue - [X] I have searched existing issues - [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly) ### Area admin/ui ### Describe the bug We've noticed this happening since our upgrade to Keycloak 22(.0.3), but it could be older. When clicking on "Join Groups" in the Admin UI, either within the user creation page or on the Groups tab of a user's page, and searching for a group, the results screen doesn't show the proper amount of results per page, doesn't report the proper amount of results, and pagination is greyed out. Location of the two Join Groups buttons we're talking about. ### Version 22.0.3, but tested on last night's nightly ### Expected behavior When clicking Join groups and search for `child`, we see the first 10 matching results. We can go to the next page to see the next 10, and the next page to see the remaining 5. ### Actual behavior When clicking Join groups and search for `Child`, we see the first 18 results, 15 of which are matching and the remaining 3 parents of the matching results (but not matching themselves). The page arrow buttons are greyed out and cannot be used. The page also reports 5 results being returned. Results shown when asking for 10 items. Note the page buttons being greyed out. ### How to Reproduce? 1. Spin-up an instance of Keycloak nightly 2. Create a new realm 3. Create the following group structure: - A - 'Child of A-`x`' for x=1 through 5 (5 subgroups in total) - B - 'Child of B-`x`' for x=1 through 5 (5 subgroups in total) - C ... E with 5 child subgroups each - F ... I with no subgroups 4. Create a user 5. Click Join groups on either the user creation page or the user's page - Groups tab. ### Anything else? This is loosely related to https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/23582 and https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/23707, but I believe is its own issue. If necessary, I can also split this one into multiple issues like: - Join group screen does not respect pagination settings with a filter - Join group screen does not allow pagination with a filter - Join group screen shows non-matching results with a filter - Join group screen reports wrong number of results with a filter

issue_title: Client assertion used to broker has invalid ES256, ES384, ES512 signatures
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

identity-brokering

### Describe the bug

When the identity provider is configured to use the ES256, ES384 or ES512 client assertion signature algorithm the assertion will be rejected due to an invalid signature.

Due to the use of a hardcoded `AsymmetricSignatureProvider` the signature being generated is DER encoded instead of the concatenated R and S values.

### Version

22.0.4

### Expected behavior

The signature for ES256, ES384 or ES512 should be the concatenated R and S values to be accepted by other compliant implementations.

### Actual behavior

The signature for ES256, ES384 or ES512 is DER encoded is and rejected by compliant implementations.

### How to Reproduce?

Configure the identity provider to use the ES256, ES384 or ES512 client assertion signature algorithm for authentication and attempt to authenticate.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Fix client assertion with invalid ES256, ES384, ES512 signatures

Closes #23721

issue_title: OpenAPI spec doesn't match the admin API
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

The OpenAPI spec generated since #20669 doesn't actually match the admin API. I've found multiple issues, there may be more I haven't found yet. These are most likely issues with the OpenAPI generator not handling types correctly and may be fixed by more annotations.

- `GET /admin/realms/{realm}` returns a `RealmRepresentation` which contains the `clientProfiles` and `clientPolicies` properties. These are defined as `JsonNode` in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/d0fed02e1d59dfc98c8ecce4b4ab2332705f47ca/core/src/main/java/org/keycloak/representations/idm/RealmRepresentation.java#L156 which could be any JSON type (and in this specific case is actually a JSON object). The OpenAPI spec defines these two properties to be arrays without any specific element type though (`"clientProfiles": {"type": "array"}`).
- `GET /admin/realms/{realm}/roles` returns a stream of roles (see https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/d0fed02e1d59dfc98c8ecce4b4ab2332705f47ca/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/admin/RoleContainerResource.java#L109) which is serialized as a JSON array of objects, but according to the OpenAPI spec, this endpoint returns an unspecified object (`"schema": {"type": "object"}`). This is most likely due to `Stream<RoleRepresentation>` not being handled correctly by the OpenAPI generator.

### Version

22.0.1

### Expected behavior

The generated OpenAPI spec should match the admin API.

### Actual behavior

The generated OpenAPI spec doesn't match the admin API and an API client generated from the spec runs into deserialization issues when calling API endpoints.

### How to Reproduce?

- Generate the OpenAPI spec using `mvn -pl services -am -DskipTests -P jboss-release`
- Generate an API client from the spec in `services/target/apidocs-rest/swagger/apidocs/openapi.json`
- Call the given API endpoints using the generated client
- Deserialization into generated types fails because the OpenAPI spec doesn't match what the API actually returns

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Fix generated OpenAPI spec

Changes:
- update the smallrye openapi generator plugin to latest to correctly handle
  Stream<T> responses
- add annotations to RealmRepresentation.clientProfiles and .clientPolicies
